data.name: textBox1.Text
data.value:
Mbot : The concept of the campaign Tomsk : PruneScript Zarbas : choice of mission Eagle : debugging and creating your own campaigns Norman99 : Fuel Check script
All the talented skin designers who help us make our campaigns more immersive

data.name: textBox_ASTI.Text
data.value:
with DCS (the mission editor) it's almost impossible to import templates with a large number of statics, and even more impossible to move or orientate them or even delete them. This is why Cef wanted a tool to do all these things: import one or more templates made of Static, orientate them, move them, delete them, whatever the original MAP.
To do this, you need to identify the location(s) where you want to place these templates using a ‘marker’ whose name begins with ASTI_ .
To increase precision, you can rename a group in the template file(s) so that it becomes the template's point of origin. Rename it starting with REF_.
Step 1: [optional] [in the template file] define a group/unit that will be the template's point of origin. To do this, rename the name of the group (not the unit) so that it begins with REF_.
Step 2: [in the target mission file (.miz)] define one or more markers (any group) for which you will start the group name (not the unit) with ASTI_, if you want to call the same template several times on different locations, do it in the following way: group.name = ‘ASTI_nameOftemplate-1’. group.name = ‘ASTI_nameOftemplate-2 and so on. The orientation and hidden of the marker group will automatically be transmitted to the copied template.
Step 3: [on the right, with the ‘select templates folder’ button]. select a folder where you have placed all the templates you wish to use.
Step 4: [right, with the button ‘select mission file .miz’] select your mission file .miz to which you want to paste the template(s)
Step 5: [right, using the ‘Process’ button]. make the modification. You can then open the file with the mission editor, make a few adjustments (moving/orienting/hiding the marker) and repeat the operation. Deleting the marker will delete the associated template.
nota: - the countries used will automatically be CJTF Blue and CJTF Red. This will avoid any conflict ^^ - unitId, groupId, group.name and unit.name will automatically be renamed to avoid duplication. - you can use Tools/ASTI independently of DCE, no need to fill in the path on the first tab.
